description: A comprehensive guide to building AI Agents with ADK-Go, scalable from absolute beginners to Senior Go Engineers.

user_personas.description: Has never written a line of code. Needs terminal and basic programming concepts.
Experienced in Python/JS, but new to Go and new to ADK.
Proficient in Go (structs, pointers, interfaces). Just needs to learn ADK.

skills.title: Installing Go and setting the GOPATH
skills.description: Downloading the Go binary, running the installer, and verifying the installation.

verification.instructions_to_user:
1. Open your terminal.
2. Run `go version` and paste the exact output here.
3. Run `go env GOPATH` and paste the exact output here.
verification.validation_script:
from user_chat_history import latest_user_message
assert "go version go1." in latest_user_message, "Go is not installed or not in PATH."
assert "GOPATH" not in latest_user_message or "/" in latest_user_message or "\\" in latest_user_message, "GOPATH is not set correctly."

skills.title: Navigating the Terminal and 'go run'
skills.description: Creating folders, navigating, and executing a simple Go script.

verification.instructions_to_user:
1. Create a folder named `hello_adk` and `cd` into it.
2. Run `go mod init hello_adk`.
3. Run `go run google.golang.org/adk/examples/hello@latest`.
4. Paste the output here.
verification.validation_script:
from user_chat_history import latest_user_message
assert "Hello," in latest_user_message, "User could not execute the remote Go script."

skills.title: Variables, Types, and Functions in Go
skills.description: Strict typing vs dynamic typing, how to write basic functions.
skills.fast_track_assessment: Write a Go function `Multiply(a int, b int) int` that returns the product.

verification.validation_script:
// Simulated test execution of user code
assert Multiply(3, 4) == 12

skills.title: Go Structs (Grouping Data)
skills.description: Crucial because ADK uses Structs heavily for Agent configuration and State.
skills.fast_track_assessment: How do you define a struct named `AgentConfig` with a `ModelName` string field?

verification.validation_script: // Simulated check that AgentConfig struct exists and has ModelName field

skills.title: Pointers (& and *)
skills.description: ADK-Go often passes State or Session context by pointer to allow modification.
skills.fast_track_assessment: What is the difference between passing `State` and `*State` to a function in terms of mutability?

pass_criteria: Mentions memory addresses, avoiding copying the whole object, or that * allows modifying the original data.

skills.title: Go Interfaces
skills.description: ADK-Go uses interfaces to define Tools (e.g., fulfilling the Tool interface vs just passing a function callback).
skills.fast_track_assessment: Does a Go struct need to use an 'implements' keyword to satisfy an interface?

skills.title: Clients, Servers, and the MCP Paradigm
skills.description: High-level concept: AI Agents act as Clients asking external Servers (Tools) for data.
skills.fast_track_assessment: In the context of the Model Context Protocol (MCP), is the LLM usually the Server exposing tools, or the Client requesting them?

skills.title: Core Primitives of ADK
skills.description: Understanding Agent, Tool, Session, State, and Runner primitives conceptually.
skills.fast_track_assessment: In ADK, what is the conceptual difference between the 'Session' (and Events) versus the 'State'?

pass_criteria: Events/Session track the conversation history, State tracks working memory/variables.

skills.title: Initializing the LlmAgent
skills.description: Importing ADK, setting up the Gemini API key, and instantiating an LlmAgent.

verification.instructions_to_user: Write a Go script that initializes a basic ADK `LlmAgent` using `gemini-2.0-flash`.

skills.title: Creating FunctionTools
skills.description: Wrapping a standard Go function (like fetching weather) into an ADK Tool so the LLM can call it.

verification.instructions_to_user: Create a simple `GetWeather` Fake Tool and attach it to your LlmAgent.

skills.title: Orchestration with the Runner
skills.description: Using the Runner engine to manage the execution loop (LLM -> Tool -> LLM -> Output).

verification.instructions_to_user: Write the `Runner.Run()` block to execute your LlmAgent and ask it for the weather.

skills.title: Agent Modes: LlmAgent vs Workflow Agents
skills.description: Understanding that LlmAgents are dynamic/non-deterministic, whereas Workflow agents (Sequential, Parallel, Loop) are fixed routing rails.
skills.fast_track_assessment: If I want three agents to gather research simultaneously before passing their results to a final summarizer, which two workflow agents would I use?

pass_criteria: ParallelAgent (for the research) and SequentialAgent (to route to the summarizer)

skills.title: Building a SequentialAgent
skills.description: Chaining Agent A -> Agent B -> Agent C in a fixed pipeline.

verification.instructions_to_user: Create two simple LlmAgents and put them into a SequentialAgent pipeline.

skills.title: Building a ParallelAgent
skills.description: Executing tasks concurrently. Excellent for fan-out tasks like analyzing multiple documents.

verification.instructions_to_user: Create a ParallelAgent containing two sub-agents.

skills.title: Building a LoopAgent
skills.description: Executing an agent repeatedly until a specific condition in the State breaks the loop.

verification.instructions_to_user: Create a LoopAgent that runs 3 times before breaking.
